req.title: Overdraft protection (negative variant)
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-062 mandates overdraft protection via a linked savings account. The test suite covers: (1) EP positive — a normal overdraft draw where savings has sufficient funds, fee is assessed, and transaction is approved; (2) NEG negative — savings balance is insufficient so the transaction is declined; (3) BVA boundary — the debit amount exactly equals checking balance plus savings balance (last possible cent), probing the maximum-draw edge; (4) DT decision-table — unauthorised account (no linked savings) is declined; (5) STC state-transition — a previously declined overdraft is retried after savings are topped up, verifying the state machine transitions from DECLINED to APPROVED. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that reset account state before and after execution.

tests.name: test_overdraft_draw_from_savings_success
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $50.00
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $120.00 against the checking account
expected: Savings balance is debited $70.00 (shortfall) + $10.00 fee = $120.00 net
trace: R-FIN-062: transaction approved when savings sufficient
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_draw_from_savings_success(api_client, reset_accounts):
    reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=50.00,
        savings_balance=200.00,
        overdraft_enabled=True,
        overdraft_fee=10.00
    )
    payload = {"account_id": "<checking_acct_id>", "amount": 120.00}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "APPROVED"
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] == 10.00
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/<checking_acct_id>/balances").json()
    assert balances["checking"] == 0.00
    assert balances["savings"] == pytest.approx(120.00, abs=0.01)

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_insufficient
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $10.00
Debit amount $80.00 exceeds combined balance of $40.00
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $80.00 against the checking account
trace: R-FIN-062: error pathway — declined when linked balance insufficient
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_insufficient(api_client, reset_accounts):
    reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=10.00,
        savings_balance=30.00,
        overdraft_enabled=True
    )
    payload = {"account_id": "<checking_acct_id>", "amount": 80.00}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "DECLINED"
    assert body["error_code"] == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/<checking_acct_id>/balances").json()
    assert balances["checking"] == 10.00
    assert balances["savings"] == 30.00

tests.name: test_overdraft_exactly_at_combined_balance_limit
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $25.00
Overdraft fee is $0.00 (waived for boundary test isolation)
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount exactly $100.00 (the maximum drawable amount)
expected: No funds remain in either account
trace: R-FIN-062: boundary — debit equals combined available balance (last valid cent)
R-FIN-062: linked savings fully exhausted but not exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_exactly_at_combined_balance_limit(api_client, reset_accounts):
    reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=25.00,
        savings_balance=75.00,
        overdraft_enabled=True,
        overdraft_fee=0.00
    )
    payload = {"account_id": "<checking_acct_id>", "amount": 100.00}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "APPROVED"
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/<checking_acct_id>/balances").json()
    assert balances["checking"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=0.01)
    assert balances["savings"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=0.01)

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_no_linked_savings_account
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $20.00
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $50.00 against the checking account
trace: R-FIN-062: negative path — unauthorised (no linked account)
R-FIN-062: decision rule — overdraft protection must be enabled with linked account
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_declined_no_linked_savings_account(api_client, reset_accounts):
    reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=20.00,
        savings_balance=None,
        overdraft_enabled=False
    )
    payload = {"account_id": "<checking_acct_id>", "amount": 50.00}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "DECLINED"
    assert body["error_code"] == "OVERDRAFT_NOT_AUTHORISED"
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/<checking_acct_id>/balances").json()
    assert balances["checking"] == 20.00

tests.name: test_overdraft_state_transition_declined_then_approved_after_savings_top_up
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance $5.00
First debit of $60.00 will be DECLINED (savings insufficient)
Savings account is then topped up to $100.00 via admin fixture
Second debit of $60.00 is within new combined limit
expected: First call returns HTTP 422 with status DECLINED
Second call returns HTTP 200 with status APPROVED
State machine transitions from DECLINED to APPROVED after balance restored
Savings debited $55.00 (shortfall) + fee on second call
trace: R-FIN-062: state transition — DECLINED state resolves to APPROVED when savings topped up
R-FIN-062: triggers — debit exceeds checking balance in both calls
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_state_transition_declined_then_approved_after_savings_top_up(
    api_client, reset_accounts, top_up_savings
):
    reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=5.00,
        savings_balance=10.00,
        overdraft_enabled=True,
        overdraft_fee=5.00
    )
    payload = {"account_id": "<checking_acct_id>", "amount": 60.00}
    resp_first = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp_first.status_code == 422
    assert resp_first.json()["status"] == "DECLINED"
    top_up_savings(account_id="<savings_acct_id>", new_balance=100.00)
    resp_second = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp_second.status_code == 200
    assert resp_second.json()["status"] == "APPROVED"
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/<checking_acct_id>/balances").json()
    assert balances["checking"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=0.01)
